info.title: ModelOps User API
info.description: ModelOps exposes a RESTful API interface with secured endpoints, accessible exclusively within the Maximus VPN. All communications are encrypted and authenticated to ensure safe interaction with AI services and resources.

servers.description: Access Token for Client Credentials API
Access Token for User Credentials API

post.description: Authorize with Basic (username/password) in the modal, then Execute to receive a token. Use that token in 'Bearer (Client Token)'.
Post user credentials in the body to receive a token. Use that token in 'Bearer (User Token)' and provide tenant_Id in the Authorize modal.

errorMessage.example: Incorrect username or password / User pool client xxxxx does not exist.

post.description: Provides granular control over model behavior, including inference configuration and multimodal input.

requestBody.description: Create a new payload for bedrock model

post.description: Allows users to configure Textract-specific features and queries for document analysis.

requestBody.description: Create a new payload for textract model

errorMessage.example: Max tokens must be between 1 and 200000.

post.description: Ideal for basic text generation tasks. Requires only a prompt; default model configurations can be overridden if needed.

requestBody.description: Create a new payload for bedrock model

post.description: With a set of queries users can extract the data given in a document.

requestBody.description: Create a new payload for textract model

errorMessage.example: Model throttle exception: An error occurred (ValidationException) when calling the InvokeModel operation: messages.0.content.0.image.source.base64: image cannot be empty.

post.summary: Prepare presigned URL for document upload
post.description: Returns a presigned URL for uploading a document to S3. Use the returned `url` to upload the file directly.

parameters.name: modelId
parameters.description: The ID of the model to invoke(textract)

post.description: Invokes Comprehend service for NLP tasks such as entity detection, sentiment analysis, or language detection.

Use this API endpoint to invoke a workflow as a tenant/user. The request is processed asynchronously: ModelOps returns a workflowJobId, runs the workflow as per its configured dispatch, and once completed you can retrieve results via the Get Data API (after checking job status).

parameters.name: workflow_id
parameters.description: The ID of the workflow to invoke

errorMessage.example: No workflow found for workflow_name: xxxx

get.summary: Get workflow details of a specific workflow
get.description: Returns details for the specified workflow ID.
Returns all workflows details of the provided tenant.

Use this API to retrieve the latest public key used by ModelOps for verifying the authenticity of callback signatures. This key is essential for validating signatures in secure communication workflows.
Provides a quick way to check if the ModelOps APIs and services are operational.
Retrieve a list of jobs created by the authenticated user or client. Supports optional query parameters for filtering by model type, job status, and more.

get.summary: Get Job by Job ID API
get.description: Retrieve detailed status and metadata for a specific job using its jobId.
Fetch the output data of a completed job. This API should be called only after the job status is marked as 'Completed'.
Returns all AI models available for the tenant.
Provides detailed information about a specific AI model, helping users assess its capabilities and integration requirements.

delete.description: Cancel a job before the jobStatus enters the Processing state, i.e. it is either in Open or Queued state. Useful for aborting incorrect or unintended submissions.

bearerAuth.description: Paste the token you obtained from the authentication API

basicAuth.description: Use only for calling the Client Credentials API to obtain a token.

prompt.example: HI what is weather today in bangalore
